# Extração: 06.04-Paid Email Drops

- **Fonte:** 06.04-Paid Email Drops.docx

Paid Email Drops



What it is?



Paying for ad space in another company’s email list (show all examples)



Newsmax

Glenn Beck

Alt Daily



Most of these lists are NOT targeted “weight loss” or “financial” or anything like that.  They’re mostly people over the age of 55.







Offers that I’ve seen work on paid email drops

Health (weight loss, diabetes, blood pressure, keto, supplements etc…)

Financial (Agora Financial, Stansberry, gold offers)

survival /Guns  (survival food, generators, water filters)

Online privacy/look up records

Political offers (i.e. a book about Trump, or a book that hates on Hillary Clinton)



Why you want to do email drops?

Email buyers are usually worth a LOT more than Facebook buyers

These buyers regularly open emails, so they’ll buy more on your backend

There is a LARGE amount of email lists you can do paid drops on (hundreds of lists)

Once you have a good email creative you can run the same creative on different lists

Allows you to expand beyond affiliates



How it works (show example)

You buy ad space on a list for a set number of subscribers (partial or full)

You give them the email creative you want them to use

You “ok” the proof

They send it out, and you see if it works or not



How to start with these

Like everything, test SMALL

A full list might be $8,000 to send to - ask if you can test $2000 to a quarter of the list

Do your research - get on these lists and see which offers are running over and over again

If you want to do this on the cheap, test your emails to your own list and with affiliates first to  find a winner.  Then test it on a paid drop (Note: we always did it the opposite way and tested on paid email drops first, then gave winning email creatives to affiliates.  If you have money and know what youre doing, this is a good way to do it)



Working with a list broker

Brokers represent hundreds of lists.  They can help guide you to the right lists that are working now (lists dont always perform the same if the list owner is NOT constantly adding new leads to it)

Scott Smoliak - scott@emailabilities.com 

Drew Kossoff - drew@rainmakeradventures.com 



If you’re doing a lot of email buying, you’ll probably work with multiple brokers





Things you need to know…

Your email creative will burn out and underperform if you just send the same creative over and over with the same subject line (show Greens examples)

If you have 2 email controls this is best (with multiple subject lines)

Most email creatives will last 4-6 months if you have multiple subject lines

Can always retire the creative for a while, then try to bring it back a year later

Always be testing a portion of your spend to find your NEXT email creative (your current one will die, so you want a backup ready to go)





Once you start testing...

Track everything in a spreadsheet (date sent, opens, clicks, sales, rev etc…)

Double down on the winners (buy more sends), leave the losers behind

Some lists might work for one of your offers, but suck for another offer of yours.

Know what numbers you want to hit - some people want to break even, some want to profit, some are ok with losing 30%.  You have to know what you’re ok with.





Once you get rolling...

  

You can negotiate your buys once you know how offers do.  I.e. we’ll buy 2 a month from you as long as it’s hitting x numbers, and you sell it to us for $x,xxx



If a list doesnt perform (especially if it usually does) ask for a make-up send
